subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Per Archon Deck
- **36 cards** — Each deck is unique and procedurally generated.
- **3 houses** — Each deck contains cards from exactly 3 of the available houses (12 cards per house).
- **Archon card** — Identity card showing the deck's unique name and house composition.
- **Card types** — Creatures, Artifacts, Actions, Upgrades.

#### Shared Components
- **Key tokens** — 6 key tokens (3 per player, in two colors).
- **Aember tokens** — Pool of Aember tokens for gaining and spending.
- **Damage tokens** — Track damage on creatures.
- **Stun cards** — Status effect markers.
- **Power counters** — Modify creature power.
- **Chain tracker** — Cards or dial for tracking chains (handicap system).

#### Available Houses (varies by set)
- **Examples** — Brobnar, Dis, Logos, Mars, Sanctum, Shadows, Untamed, Star Alliance, Saurian, and others depending on set.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Each player takes a deck** — Players use their own unique Archon deck; no deckbuilding.
2. **Place Archon card** — Set identity card face up.
3. **Unforged keys** — Place 3 unforged key tokens in front of each player.
4. **Supply tokens** — Place Aember, damage, stun, and power tokens in shared supply.
5. **No shared board** — Each player has their own battleline (row of creatures) and artifact area.
6. **Shuffle decks** — Each player shuffles their 36-card deck.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Hand
- **First player** — Draws 7 cards, then must mulligan to 6 cards (mandatory for balance).
- **Second player** — Draws 7 cards; may optionally mulligan (shuffle all back, draw 7 again, then discard 1).
- **No partial mulligan** — Mulligan is all-or-nothing.

#### Battleline
- **Creatures** — Played to left or right flank of your battleline.
- **Artifacts** — Placed in a separate row.
- **Aember pool** — Track your current Aember.

#### First Turn Restriction
- **First player limitation** — On their first turn, the first player can only play or discard 1 card.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Determine first player** — Use any random method.
2. **Apply chains** — If using the chain handicap system, apply chains before drawing.
3. **Draw starting hands** — First player draws 7 (keeps 6); second player draws 7.
4. **First player takes turn** — With the first-turn play restriction.
5. **Alternate turns** — Players alternate full turns.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Steps (in order)
1. **Forge a key** — If you have 6+ Aember, you must forge a key (spend 6 Aember, flip a key token).
2. **Choose a house** — Declare one of your deck's 3 houses as your active house for this turn.
3. **Play, discard, and use cards** — Play any number of cards from the active house; use any number of active-house creatures/artifacts already in play; discard active-house cards from hand.
4. **Ready cards** — Ready all of your exhausted cards.
5. **Draw cards** — Draw back up to 6 cards.

#### Key Rule
- **Mandatory forge** — Forging is mandatory at start of turn if you have enough Aember.
- **Key cost** — Default is 6 Aember; some card effects modify this.
- **Victory** — First player to forge 3 keys wins immediately.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Playing Cards
- **Creatures** — Enter play exhausted on a flank (left or right end of battleline).
- **Artifacts** — Enter play ready in your artifact area.
- **Actions** — Resolve immediately and discard.
- **Upgrades** — Attach to a creature, modifying it.

#### Using Creatures
- **Reap** — Exhaust a ready creature to gain 1 Aember. Triggers "Reap:" abilities.
- **Fight** — Exhaust a ready creature to fight an enemy creature. Both deal their power as damage simultaneously.
- **Action ability** — Exhaust to use a creature's "Action:" ability.
- **Stun interaction** — Using a stunned creature removes the stun instead of performing the action.

#### Using Artifacts
- **Action ability** — Exhaust to use the artifact's "Action:" ability.
- **Omni ability** — Some artifacts have "Omni:" abilities usable even when their house is not active.

#### Combat Details
- **Mutual damage** — Both fighting creatures deal damage equal to their power simultaneously.
- **Destruction** — A creature with damage equal to or exceeding its power is destroyed.
- **Taunt** — Creatures with taunt must be attacked before non-taunt creatures.
- **Elusive** — Elusive creatures take no damage the first time they are attacked each turn.
- **Skirmish** — Creature deals damage in a fight but does not receive damage.

#### Aember Control
- **Steal** — Take Aember from opponent's pool and add to yours.
- **Capture** — Place Aember from opponent's pool on your creature; returns to opponent when creature is destroyed.
- **Aember bonus** — Cards with Aember icons on them grant that much Aember when played.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Unique Deck System
- **No deckbuilding** — Every deck is played exactly as printed; no modifications allowed.
- **Procedural generation** — Each deck is algorithmically generated with a unique name and card combination.
- **Deck identity** — The Archon card and QR code identify the deck for organized play.

#### Chains (Handicap System)
- **Chain count** — Chains reduce your hand size when drawing.
- **Shedding chains** — Each time you draw fewer cards due to chains, remove 1 chain.
- **Application** — Used to balance decks of different power levels.
- **Draw penalty** — Chains 1-6: draw 1 fewer; Chains 7-12: draw 2 fewer; and so on.

#### House Selection Strategy
- **All-or-nothing** — You can only interact with cards from your chosen house each turn.
- **Off-house cards** — Cards from non-active houses sit idle in hand and in play.
- **Omni exception** — Cards with "Omni:" abilities can be used regardless of active house.

#### Card Abilities
- **Play:** — Triggers when the card enters play.
- **Reap:** — Triggers when the creature reaps.
- **Fight:** — Triggers when the creature fights.
- **Destroyed:** — Triggers when the card is destroyed.
- **Before Fight:** — Resolves before combat damage.
- **Action:** — Activated by exhausting the card.
- **Omni:** — Usable regardless of active house (still exhausts).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **Forge 3 keys** — First player to forge their third key wins immediately.
- **Forge timing** — Keys are forged at the start of your turn before any other actions.

#### Key Cost
- **Default cost** — 6 Aember per key.
- **Modified cost** — Some card effects increase or decrease key cost.

#### No Points System
- **Race format** — The game is a race to 3 keys; there is no VP tally.
- **No tiebreaker** — Only one player forges at a time (start of their turn), so ties are impossible.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Identify your deck's strengths** — Determine if your deck excels at Aember generation, board control, or disruption.
- **Establish board presence** — Play creatures early to create reap opportunities in subsequent turns.
- **Choose house with most impact** — Select the house that develops your board or generates the most Aember.
- **Track opponent's Aember** — Monitor when they approach 6 to prepare steal or capture responses.
- **Use the first-turn restriction wisely** — First player should play their most impactful single card.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring opponent's Aember** — Letting the opponent accumulate 6+ Aember without disruption leads to quick key forges.
- **Fighting when you should reap** — Unnecessary fights waste creatures; reap to generate Aember unless combat is essential.
- **Poor house selection** — Choosing a house with few cards in hand or in play wastes a turn.
- **Hoarding cards** — Holding cards in hand from non-active houses is fine, but never playing them is wasteful.
- **Forgetting mandatory forge** — You must forge at the start of your turn if you have 6+ Aember; missing it is an illegal play.
- **Misplacing creatures** — Flank placement matters for cards with positional abilities.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Reap vs fight** — Generate Aember for yourself or eliminate a threatening creature.
- **House selection** — Balance between progressing your board and responding to opponent threats.
- **When to go wide vs tall** — More creatures means more reap opportunities; fewer stronger creatures resist removal.
- **Aember burst vs steady generation** — Save high-Aember turns for when opponent cannot respond, or spread generation evenly.
- **Defensive play near 3 keys** — When opponent is at 2 keys, prioritize Aember control over your own generation.
